{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether adjuvant radiation is necessary for stage III thymoma. Multiple documents support that adjuvant radiotherapy is indicated for stage III thymomas. Document [3] states that in stage II-III thymomas, PORT is indicated due to the higher recurrence risk. Document [2] explicitly recommends adjuvant radiotherapy for patients with stage III or IV thymomas, even with complete resection. Document [1] notes that a SEER database analysis showed superior overall and disease-free survival in patients with thymomas who received postoperative radiotherapy, with the benefit limited to stage III and IV disease. Document [9] and [10] also support the use of postoperative radiotherapy in advanced-stage disease. While some studies show conflicting results (e.g., JART found no survival benefit in thymic carcinoma), the majority of evidence, including retrospective reviews and clinical guidelines, supports the use of adjuvant radiation in stage III thymoma due to higher recurrence risk and improved outcomes. Therefore, based on the available evidence, adjuvant radiation is considered necessary for stage III thymoma.", "answer_choice": "A"}
